title: Production Edge Agentic AI for Mining Safety
category: Agentic AI + Edge AI + Computer Vision
one_liner: Designed real-time on-device agentic AI systems for safety-critical video analytics on NVIDIA Jetson AGX.
problem: Mining and industrial environments require continuous safety monitoring, but manual monitoring is expensive, slow, and error-prone.
solution: Built multimodal edge AI pipelines combining perception, reasoning, and action loops for real-time video analytics under strict latency, compute, power, and reliability constraints.
technical_details: Computer vision pipelines for hazard detection and workflow automation
Edge-aware optimization for latency, memory, and throughput
Client-facing delivery with stakeholder demos and implementation roadmaps
suggested_visuals: Pipeline flow: video stream → perception → reasoning → action
recruiter_angle: Shows rare combination of agentic AI, computer vision, edge deployment, and production constraints.

title: Video-RAG for Continuous Video Search
one_liner: Architected retrieval-oriented video understanding pipelines for natural-language search over live and long-horizon video streams.
problem: Long video streams are difficult to search, reason over, and summarize using traditional indexing approaches.
solution: Built a Video-RAG pipeline using compressed spatio-temporal embeddings, CLIP/VLM-derived representations, semantic text embeddings, multimodal indexing, and LLM reasoning.
impact: Sub-second search latency over multi-day indexed video
Contextual querying and decision support over live video streams
technical_details: TensorRT / FP16 optimization on Jetson AGX Orin
recruiter_angle: Strong differentiator for GenAI, RAG, multimodal AI, and applied research roles.

title: Enterprise AI for Sprint Planning and Resource Allocation
one_liner: Developed an agentic planning workflow to support prioritization, sequencing, trade-off analysis, and resource allocation.
problem: Product, engineering, and operations teams need better visibility into dependencies, constraints, and competing priorities during planning.
solution: Formalized product definitions, feature requirements, sprint goals, and workflow logic into structured context for AI-assisted decision support.
impact: Improved visibility into dependencies and constraints
Framed as a human-AI collaboration system for workflow redesign
technical_details: Research framing around organizational AI adoption
recruiter_angle: Best case study for AI consulting, strategy, forward-deployed AI, and product-facing AI roles.

title: High-Performance Inference and MLOps for Real-Time AI
one_liner: Built GPU-accelerated inference and deployment pipelines that improved throughput, latency, reproducibility, and validation.
problem: Production AI workloads need predictable latency, efficient memory usage, reproducible deployment, and continuous validation.
solution: Engineered real-time and batch inference pipelines using ONNX, TensorRT, CUDA, GStreamer, MLflow, Docker, AWS, CI/CD, and edge-device runners.
recruiter_angle: Targets ML systems, infra, edge AI, and performance engineering recruiters.

title: Real-Time Infrastructure Defect Detection
one_liner: Built real-time anomaly detection and inspection systems integrating live drone feeds, ML predictions, and analytics dashboards.
problem: Infrastructure inspection workflows are slow and costly when dependent on manual review.
solution: Developed ensemble ML models and real-time analytics pipelines for structural defect classification.
recruiter_angle: Proves applied ML product delivery before JSquared.

title: LLM-Based Pull Request Review Assistant
one_liner: Built an LLM-based pull request review system to automate code analysis and improve engineering efficiency.
problem: Code review can be slow, inconsistent, and difficult to scale across fast-moving AI development teams.
solution: Implemented an LLM-driven review workflow to analyze PRs, surface issues, and support engineering quality.
impact: Supported faster applied AI development workflows
recruiter_angle: Good supporting project for AI software engineer and internal tools roles.

title: FalcHub — Edge-to-Cloud File Sync Sidecar
one_liner: Designed and built FalcHub, a production edge sidecar (Python) that reliably syncs GB-scale daily file volume from field devices to AWS S3 over unreliable networks — using a concurrent multi-process pipeline, certificate-based IoT auth, and crash-safe atomic state.
problem: Unattended edge devices in industrial environments produce GB-scale file volume every day and must upload it to the cloud continuously for months — over decent-but-unreliable LAN/WiFi links that drop frequently — surviving crashes, power loss, and outages without ever losing or double-uploading data, and without storing any static cloud credentials on the device.
solution: Built a fork-based supervisor process that isolates three worker processes (file discovery, upload, and cleanup) communicating only through a shared SQLite ledger with atomic, crash-safe state transitions. High per-device file volume is absorbed by a concurrent pipeline that keeps scanning, uploading, and retention from blocking each other; files upload to AWS S3 using short-lived credentials obtained via AWS IoT X.509 certificate authentication, are confirmed with a backend API, and are deleted only after a retention-gated, confirmed sync.
impact: GB-scale files synced per device, every day
3 isolated worker processes for concurrent, non-blocking throughput
Zero data loss across crashes and unreliable-network drops
0 static AWS keys — short-lived IoT credentials only
technical_details: Concurrency via process isolation: independent Walker/Uploader/Cleaner workers so high per-device file volume never blocks scanning, upload, or retention — sustaining throughput on constrained edge hardware
Resilient to unreliable LAN/WiFi: bounded retries with a transport-vs-permanent failure classifier and a watchdog that reclaims stalled uploads, so files still reach S3 despite frequent link drops
Efficient at scale: an atomic compare-and-swap claim ensures workers never contend or re-upload a file, and per-process credential caching avoids repeated STS round-trips
Fork-safe supervisor (SystemOrchestrator) with restart-storm throttling and a staged shutdown ladder for months of unattended operation
Zero-ambient-credentials model: every S3 client requires explicit IoT-issued temporary credentials via an X.509 → role-alias → STS exchange

Two-stage Docker build compiling a patched SQLite (SHA3-256 pinned) to fix a WAL-checkpoint bug, enforced by a boot-time and build-time safety gate; Jenkins CI/CD with cosign signing + SPDX SBOM attestation to Harbor/ECR
suggested_visuals: File lifecycle state machine (pending → uploading → synced/failed)
Zero-credential IoT → STS credential exchange flow
recruiter_angle: Demonstrates high-volume, constrained-network edge throughput plus production security and supply-chain depth — concurrent crash-safe process design and end-to-end signed/attested delivery, owned solo.

title: VisitorLog — Multi-Site Visitor Management Platform
one_liner: Architected and built VisitorLog end to end — a multi-site platform serving many kiosks and concurrent approvers with low-latency check-in, signed QR badge credentials, and an efficient, horizontally scalable zero-trust FastAPI + Next.js + PostgreSQL stack.
problem: A multi-site organization needed to replace manual, ad-hoc visitor tracking with a governed workflow that serves many kiosks and tens of concurrent approvers across sites — routing each of hundreds of daily visits through the correct security approval based on classification, protecting sensitive PII, and producing a complete, auditable record of every visit and system access, all without sacrificing kiosk responsiveness.
solution: Designed a horizontally scalable 3-tier system (Cloudflare Access → Next.js BFF → FastAPI API → PostgreSQL) with a stateless API and pooled database connections to serve concurrent kiosks and approvers efficiently. It routes visit requests to the right approver by security classification, collects and encrypts only the PII each classification requires, and issues signed, site-scoped QR credentials that unattended kiosks verify in-process for sub-second check-in, badge printing, and checkout — all behind zero-trust, per-application edge authentication.
impact: Sub-second kiosk check-in and QR validation
technical_details: Built for horizontal scale: a stateless FastAPI service with pooled PostgreSQL connections serves many kiosks and tens of concurrent approvers across sites without per-request connection churn
Low-latency kiosk path: signed QR (VLQ1) credentials are verified cryptographically in-process — no round-trip to a token store — keeping check-in/checkout sub-second under load
Rate-limited (slowapi) public kiosk surface stays responsive and abuse-resistant while unauthenticated
Parallel Dockerized CI stages (backend pytest + frontend Vitest/tsc/ESLint) keep the pipeline fast as the codebase grows
Zero-trust, per-area JWT audience scoping so tokens from one Cloudflare Access surface cannot be replayed on another
Field-level PII encryption at rest gated by visit classification; 42-model schema carried through 18 zero-downtime Alembic migrations
suggested_visuals: 3-tier architecture: Cloudflare Access → Next.js BFF → FastAPI → PostgreSQL
Signed QR credential issuance + kiosk check-in sequence
recruiter_angle: Strong end-to-end full-stack case study — a horizontally scalable multi-site platform with low-latency kiosk UX and zero-trust security (signed credentials, encrypted PII, per-area token scoping), architected and shipped solo to v1.0.0.
